clips.speaker: William D. Cohan
clips.title: 耶鲁管理学院执行研究员：马斯克财富堪比洛克菲勒，但权力更甚
title_lines: 耶鲁管理学院执行研究员
马斯克财富堪比洛克菲勒

subtitles.en: He's an executive fellow, excuse me, at the Yale School of Management. All right, break it down.
subtitles.zh: 他是耶鲁管理学院的执行研究员。好，来分析一下。

subtitles.en: It's hard to compare Musk to kings and queens and heads of state because they had things at their disposal and hard currencies that he kind of doesn't.
subtitles.zh: 很难将马斯克与国王、女王或国家元首相提并论，因为他们拥有他无法企及的资源和硬通货。

subtitles.en: But is this kind of private citizen wealth unprecedented in the monitored, written history of the world?
subtitles.zh: 但这种私人财富规模在有记载的世界历史中是否前所未有？

subtitles.en: It's not completely unprecedented.
subtitles.zh: 并非完全前所未有。

subtitles.en: John D. Rockefeller probably controlled roughly the same size of US GDP in terms of his wealth compared to the scale of the country.
subtitles.zh: 约翰·D·洛克菲勒的财富占美国GDP的比例，大概与马斯克相当。

subtitles.en: The estimates vary, but two and a half to three percent, so he was somewhere between a little bit below Musk and a little bit above.
subtitles.zh: 估算值在2.5%到3%之间，所以他略低于或略高于马斯克。
en_highlights: two and a half to three percent

subtitles.en: The corner of the realm here is power, as you point out, in your piece.
subtitles.zh: 正如你在文章中所指出的，核心在于权力。

subtitles.en: That's what you can get if you have a lot of money.
subtitles.zh: 这就是你拥有巨额财富所能得到的。

subtitles.en: We've seen Elon Musk exercise that before he became a trillionaire.
subtitles.zh: 我们在埃隆·马斯克成为万亿富翁之前就见过他行使这种权力。

clips.speaker_context: Executive Fellow at Yale School of Management

clips.speaker: William D. Cohan
clips.title: 耶鲁研究员：马斯克用财富左右政治，慈善捐赠不足0.1%
title_lines: 马斯克用财富左右政治

subtitles.en: He bought Twitter, now X. He kind of controls the way that we talk and communicate with one another at least some of us.
subtitles.zh: 他收购了推特（现X），某种程度上控制着我们彼此交流的方式——至少对部分人而言。

subtitles.en: He spent an awful lot of money on the last election.
subtitles.zh: 他在上次选举中投入了巨额资金。

subtitles.en: He intends to do so on the next election.
subtitles.zh: 他打算在下一次选举中继续这么做。

subtitles.en: Talk a bit about the way in which a person of his means and gets a singular thing where somebody who has the amount of money that he has can wield influence.
subtitles.zh: 谈谈像他这样拥有巨额财富的人，如何能够施加影响力。

subtitles.en: Not just on building libraries, civic life, artistic life in this country, but can really kind of change the way that power is controlled in a country like the US.
subtitles.zh: 不仅仅是建设图书馆、公民生活、艺术生活，而是真正改变像美国这样的国家中权力被控制的方式。
en_highlights: change the way power is controlled

subtitles.en: Yeah, I mean, and the scale of that is it's kind of hard to process.
subtitles.zh: 是的，这种规模令人难以想象。

subtitles.en: I mean, the way I was thinking about it last night is Sergei Brenn is the second wealthiest person in the world.
subtitles.zh: 昨晚我想到，谢尔盖·布林是世界第二富有的人。

subtitles.en: And you and I are much, much, much closer to Sergei Brenn than Sergei Brenn is Delana.
subtitles.zh: 而你我和谢尔盖·布林之间的差距，远小于谢尔盖·布林与马斯克之间的差距。

subtitles.en: Cheers me, God, I'm to hear that.
subtitles.zh: 这让我感到欣慰。

subtitles.en: I think that makes me feel worse.
subtitles.zh: 我觉得这让我感觉更糟了。

subtitles.en: I don't like that.
subtitles.zh: 我不喜欢这样。

subtitles.en: And, and must himself, right?
subtitles.zh: 而马斯克本人呢？

subtitles.en: So, you know, sort of in the Andrew Carnegie tradition for the United States, people of this kind of wealth, like we sort of expected to give lots of charity.
subtitles.zh: 在美国，像安德鲁·卡内基那样的传统，拥有这种财富的人通常被期望进行大量慈善捐赠。

subtitles.en: So you said libraries, universities, things like that must doesn't do that, right?
subtitles.zh: 所以你说图书馆、大学之类的，马斯克并没有这样做，对吧？

subtitles.en: Must total charitable giving looks like something well under 0.1% of his total of his total net worth.
subtitles.zh: 马斯克的慈善捐赠总额远低于其净资产的0.1%。

subtitles.en: So what you're seeing is what you can buy with power is one way to put it is the most expensive Senate race in history.
subtitles.zh: 所以你所看到的是，用权力可以买到的东西——可以说是有史以来最昂贵的参议院竞选。

clips.speaker_context: Executive Fellow at Yale School of Management

clips.speaker: William D. Cohan
clips.title: 耶鲁研究员：马斯克一人可碾压任何参议员，财富波动如买汽水
title_lines: 马斯克一人可碾压任何参议员

subtitles.en: Share a brown versus Bernie Moreno in Ohio.
subtitles.zh: 俄亥俄州的谢罗德·布朗对阵伯尼·莫雷诺。

subtitles.en: And the two campaigns combined spent about $110, $115 million together, not any one campaign, but together.
subtitles.zh: 两个竞选团队总共花费了约1.1亿到1.15亿美元，不是单方，而是合计。

subtitles.en: The two, that's what the two campaign spent Elon Musk spent $250 million in that campaign race.
subtitles.zh: 这就是两个竞选团队的花费，而埃隆·马斯克在那场竞选中花费了2.5亿美元。

subtitles.en: So by himself, he could swamp any US Senator.
subtitles.zh: 所以他一个人就能碾压任何一位美国参议员。

subtitles.en: But the way I would put it is if you look at the day to day fluctuations in his wealth, right?
subtitles.zh: 但我想说的是，如果你看看他财富的日常波动，对吧？

subtitles.en: Just the random motions of the stock market.
subtitles.zh: 只是市场的随机波动。

subtitles.en: So he could, what he could spend in a political camp, when he spent in that political campaign, be like 1% of the day-to-day fluctuations in his wealth.
subtitles.zh: 所以他在政治竞选中的花费，可能只相当于他财富日常波动的1%。

subtitles.en: So he can swamp any Senator, any Congressman, probably any presidential candidate, about as casually as you or I would buy a soda.
subtitles.zh: 所以他可以碾压任何参议员、任何众议员，甚至任何总统候选人，就像你我买一瓶汽水一样随意。

subtitles.en: Think about that.
subtitles.zh: 想想看。

subtitles.en: Again, this segment is not making me feel better. I'm very glad to see you, but you're not shearing me up on a Saturday.
subtitles.zh: 再说一次，这段内容并没有让我感觉好一些。很高兴见到你，但你并没有在周六让我振奋起来。

clips.speaker_context: Executive Fellow at Yale School of Management

clips.speaker: William D. Cohan
clips.title: 耶鲁研究员：从克拉苏到马斯克，财富如何摧毁共和制

subtitles.en: This piece that you have coming out, you used two very different historical examples.
subtitles.zh: 你即将发表的这篇文章中，用了两个截然不同的历史例子。

subtitles.en: One is in Rome with the guy who bankrolled Julius Caesar.
subtitles.zh: 一个是罗马，资助尤利乌斯·凯撒的那个人。

subtitles.en: And the other is, at the turn of 20th century, when Teddy Roosevelt broke up JP Morgan's wealth.
subtitles.zh: 另一个是20世纪初，西奥多·罗斯福拆分JP摩根的财富。

subtitles.en: Yeah, Crassus was the wealthiest person in the end of the Roman Republic.
subtitles.zh: 是的，克拉苏是罗马共和国末期最富有的人。

subtitles.en: He bankrolled Caesar. What he basically was use his enormous wealth to, well, they preserved the forms of the Republic.
subtitles.zh: 他资助了凯撒。他基本上是利用巨额财富，嗯，他们保留了共和国的形式。

subtitles.en: So the Senate still existed, right? All of these rules were there.
subtitles.zh: 所以元老院仍然存在，对吧？所有规则都在。

subtitles.en: In practice, Crassus and his wealth and other sort of Roman oligarchs combined to bypass all those things and rule the Republic directly.
subtitles.zh: 实际上，克拉苏和他的财富以及其他罗马寡头联手绕过了这一切，直接统治了共和国。

subtitles.en: And that led quite directly to the fall of the Republic and what became the Roman Empire.
subtitles.zh: 这直接导致了共和国的灭亡，以及后来的罗马帝国。

subtitles.en: Crassus used to say that you're not really wealthy unless you can afford to pay for a private army.
subtitles.zh: 克拉苏曾说过，除非你能负担得起一支私人军队，否则你算不上真正的富有。

subtitles.en: That's what the definition of sort of real wealth.
subtitles.zh: 这就是真正财富的定义。

subtitles.en: And by that standard, you know, the United States is a lot of people was real wealth.
subtitles.zh: 按照这个标准，美国有很多人拥有真正的财富。

subtitles.en: Republics have dealt with this problem through all of history.
subtitles.zh: 共和制在历史上一直面临这个问题。

subtitles.en: And this is the center, maybe the most important way in which a Republic falls that because Republics are very good at generating private wealth.
subtitles.zh: 这是核心，也许是共和制衰亡的最重要方式——因为共和制非常擅长创造私人财富。

subtitles.en: And then people with private wealth say, well, you know, there are only so many yachts I can buy.
subtitles.zh: 然后拥有私人财富的人会说，嗯，我能买的游艇是有限的。

subtitles.en: There are only so many in the modern context private jets I can buy.
subtitles.zh: 在现代，我能买的私人飞机也是有限的。

subtitles.en: But power is something that I can buy that has no rivals, right?
subtitles.zh: 但权力是我可以买到的、没有竞争对手的东西，对吧？

clips.speaker_context: Executive Fellow at Yale School of Management

clips.speaker: William D. Cohan
clips.title: 耶鲁研究员：罗斯福如何对抗JP摩根，历史警示今日

subtitles.en: But when I have power, then I'm above the law, then I'm above everything else.
subtitles.zh: 但当我拥有权力时，我就凌驾于法律之上，凌驾于一切之上。

subtitles.en: But the United States has dealt with this issue before.
subtitles.zh: 但美国以前处理过这个问题。

subtitles.en: And so theater Roosevelt and JP Morgan, when Roosevelt began to investigate JP Morgan's trust and his sort of sort of control of the US economy.
subtitles.zh: 所以西奥多·罗斯福和JP摩根，当罗斯福开始调查摩根的信托及其对美国经济的控制时。

subtitles.en: And while Morgan himself was not that rich, his financial network was such that he really did have a level of control over the US economy that probably no individual today approaches.
subtitles.zh: 虽然摩根本人并非那么富有，但他的金融网络使他确实对美国经济拥有一定程度的控制，这种程度今天可能无人能及。

subtitles.en: Morgan said to Roosevelt, if you have a problem with what I am doing, have your man meet my man.
subtitles.zh: 摩根对罗斯福说，如果你对我的所作所为有意见，让你的人和我的人谈谈。

subtitles.en: And tell us what we're doing wrong.
subtitles.zh: 告诉我们哪里做错了。

subtitles.en: Yeah.
subtitles.zh: 是的。

subtitles.en: And by your man, he met the attorney general.
subtitles.zh: 他所说的“你的人”指的是司法部长。

subtitles.en: So JP Morgan, as you know, theater Roosevelt himself said, say that JP Morgan could not see theater Roosevelt as anything other but a rival operator of another big firm.
subtitles.zh: 所以JP摩根，如你所知，西奥多·罗斯福本人曾说，摩根只能将罗斯福视为另一家大公司的竞争对手。

clips.speaker_context: Executive Fellow at Yale School of Management
